title: Value Network — Colleges, Universities, and Professional Schools (NAICS 611310)

sections.title: Header
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **NAICS code** | 611310 — Colleges, Universities, and Professional Schools |
| **CFJ** | Deliver accredited higher education programs and credentials to enrolled students within a safe, legally compliant, and research-capable campus environment |
| **Output types** | Research University (Carnegie R1/R2 — doctoral, extensive research); Teaching-Focused University (M1/M2 — master's, mixed teaching/research); Liberal Arts College (Bac — undergraduate-only); Community / Junior College (associate degrees, certificates); Professional / Technical School (law, medicine, engineering, vocational) |
| **Hierarchy** | Process VN only (L7 → L4). No Product BOM — output is an educational outcome and credential. |
| **Scale** | ~5,300 colleges and universities in the US; ~19.7M students enrolled; 1/3 of buildings are 50+ years old; national deferred maintenance backlog >$100B (growing at 6–8% annually) |
| **Key operators** | Public research universities (Big Ten, UC System), private liberal arts colleges (NESCAC, LAC networks), community college districts, for-profit professional schools (Strayer, DeVry) |
| **Security market size** | Global school/campus security: USD 4.2B (2025). Higher education segment ~35% of total. Access control global market: USD 10.62B (2025), CAGR 8.3%. Panic & Emergency Exit Devices globally: USD 3.9B (2025). University building age (avg. 50+ years) is the primary retrofit demand driver. |
| **Architecture distance** | 2 (Rank 8 of 15 markets) — product fits standard campus doors without hardware modification; adaptations limited to campus security platform integration and range for large buildings |
| **Market priority** | HIGH |

---

sections.title: L7 — Ecosystem
sections.content:
### L7: Higher Education Delivery Ecosystem

The complete ecosystem of universities, colleges, accreditation bodies (HLC, SACSCOC, WASC), federal regulators (DOE, OCR, OSHA, NFPA), state education boards, research funders (NSF, NIH, DoD), facilities operators, security vendors, housing services, technology suppliers, and community stakeholders that co-produce academic credentials, research output, and workforce development for students. The ecosystem operates under dual mandate: fire and life-safety code compliance (NFPA 101 Life Safety Code, IBC Chapter 10 means of egress, Clery Act) AND academic accreditation standards that require documented quality of physical environment. Aging building stock (over one-third of campus buildings exceed 50 years) creates sustained retrofit demand for security, HVAC, and life-safety systems — with >$100B in deferred maintenance nationally, growing at 6–8% annually.

---

sections.title: L6 — System-of-Systems Decomposition
sections.content:
Nine L6 nodes cover the full operational process for higher education delivery. Five are core (sequential service delivery chain); four are horizontal (run in parallel across all core steps).

| L6 ID | Type | Name | Job Family | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **L6a** | Core | Admissions & Enrollment | Recruit and matriculate qualified applicants into accredited degree programs | Prospecting, application review, financial aid packaging, enrollment confirmation, student record creation, cohort planning |
| **L6b** | Core | Academic Instruction & Curriculum Delivery | Deliver accredited coursework and degree-program learning to enrolled students | Classroom instruction, seminars, online/hybrid delivery, laboratory sessions, studio and clinical instruction, academic advising |
| **L6c** | Core | Research & Scholarship | Generate, validate, and disseminate new knowledge funded by grants and institutional resources | Grant-funded lab research, publication, IP development, graduate training, research compliance (IRB, IACUC) |
| **L6d** | Core | Student Support & Campus Life | Sustain student retention, wellbeing, and social development throughout enrollment | Residence life, counseling, health services, career advising, disability services, dining, recreational facilities |
| **L6e** | Core | Assessment, Credentialing & Graduation | Evaluate academic performance and confer accredited degrees, certificates, and credentials | Grading, degree audit, registrar services, commencement, transcript issuance, alumni records |
| **L6-H1** | Horizontal | Campus Safety, Security & Emergency Management (H) | Protect all occupants and property across all campus operations while maintaining regulatory-compliant evacuation readiness at all times | Access control, emergency exit monitoring, surveillance, fire detection, mass notification, lockdown systems, Clery Act compliance documentation |
| **L6-H2** | Horizontal | Facilities & Physical Plant Management (H) | Maintain all campus buildings and infrastructure to code and operational readiness | HVAC, electrical, plumbing, custodial, grounds, deferred maintenance programs, ADA compliance, energy management |
| **L6-H3** | Horizontal | IT & Digital Infrastructure (H) | Provide reliable network, device, software, and data infrastructure for all core steps | Campus LAN/WiFi, data centers, identity management, ERP, cybersecurity, LMS and SIS platforms |
| **L6-H4** | Horizontal | Administrative, Finance & Compliance (H) | Ensure legal, accreditation, financial, and HR compliance across all university operations | HR/payroll, budget management, accreditation reporting, legal affairs, board governance, insurance, Title IX compliance |

---

sections.title: L6a — Admissions & Enrollment
sections.content:
### L5 Systems under L6a

#### L5.a.1: Admissions CRM & Application Platform

Manages prospective student pipeline from inquiry through enrollment decision. Integrates with Common App, coalition platforms, and institutional portals.

| Alternative | Market Share | Confidence |
|---|---|---|
| Slate (Technolutions) | ~55% | MEDIUM |
| Salesforce Education Cloud | ~20% | MEDIUM |
| TargetX / Campus Management | ~12% | MEDIUM |
| Hobsons / Liaison | ~8% | MEDIUM |
| In-house / other | ~5% | LOW |

| L4 Subsystem | Description |
|---|---|
| Application Review Engine | Holistic review workflow, rubric scoring, committee routing |
| Prospect & Inquiry Tracker | Lead capture, communication cadences, yield prediction |
| Financial Aid Packaging Module | Institutional aid calculation, federal aid integration (FAFSA/CPS) |
| Enrollment Confirmation Portal | Deposit collection, housing intent, orientation sign-up |

---

Central record of all enrolled students: academic standing, grades, financial aid, holds, registration. Required for federal IPEDS reporting.

| Workday Student | ~15% | MEDIUM |
| Ellucian Colleague | ~15% | MEDIUM |
| Unit4 Student / Other | ~15% | LOW |

| L4 Subsystem | Description |

| Financial Aid & Billing | Aid disbursement, billing, payment plan management |
| IPEDS / Federal Reporting Feed | Annual federal reporting, enrollment census, completion stats |
| Degree Progress Tracker | Cumulative credit audit, graduation eligibility |

---

sections.title: L6c — Research & Scholarship
sections.content:

sections.title: L6-H1 — Campus Safety, Security & Emergency Management (H)
sections.content:
*Horizontal: runs across ALL core L6 steps. Every process step depends on a safe, legally compliant, continuously monitored physical environment. NFPA 101 Life Safety Code and IBC Chapter 10 (means of egress) apply universally. Clery Act requires annual security reporting. **Türwächter entry point is at L5.h1.2 Emergency Exit Alarm Guards.***

### L5 Systems under L6-H1

#### L5.h1.1: Campus Access Control System

Electronic and mechanical credential-based access management for buildings, labs, and restricted zones across the full campus.

| Alternative | Market Share | Confidence |
|---|---|---|
| ASSA ABLOY (Aperio, Sargent) | ~25% | MEDIUM |
| Allegion / Schlage (AD-series, NDE) | ~22% | MEDIUM |
| dormakaba (Saflok, Ambiance) | ~18% | MEDIUM |
| Lenel / HID Global (ProAccess) | ~15% | MEDIUM |
| Johnson Controls (Tyco C-CURE) | ~12% | MEDIUM |
| Other / legacy | ~8% | LOW |

*Source: [Campus Safety Magazine 2024 Access Control Survey](https://www.campussafetymagazine.com/insights/2025-access-control-predictions/164974/); [ASSA ABLOY DSS Campus Security](https://www.assaabloydss.com/en/solutions/solutions-by-market/education/securing-your-college-campus)*

| L4 Subsystem | Description |
|---|---|
| Electronic Lock Hardware | Electric strikes, magnetic locks, electromechanical mortise locks; door hardware for each access point |
| Card / Mobile Credential Reader | Proximity card (125 kHz / 13.56 MHz), mobile BLE credential, biometric reader |
| Door Controller / Panel | Embedded controller managing request-to-exit, lock/unlock scheduling, audit log |
| Access Control Management Software | Credential database, zone provisioning, real-time event log, badge issuance |

---

#### L5.h1.2: Emergency Exit Alarm Guards — **Türwächter Entry Point**

Surface-mounted mechanical guard devices that prevent unauthorized use of emergency exit doors while maintaining EN 179 / EN 1125 single-motion escape capability and (in IoT variants) broadcasting door-open and alarm events to centralized campus monitoring. Installed on existing door handles — no structural modification required.

**Product fit:** GfS Türwächter (Exit Control 179/1125 with IoT) competes at this L5 position. Architecture distance = 2 (standard campus doors, standard fire codes, retrofit-compatible).

| Alternative | Market Share | Confidence |
|---|---|---|
| GfS Exit Control 179/1125 (mechanical + IoT) | ~35% | LOW |
| dormakaba SafeRoute / PaniRoute (exit alarm) | ~25% | LOW |
| GEZE SecuLogic (electronic exit control) | ~15% | LOW |
| Allegion Precision / Von Duprin (alarmed exit) | ~15% | LOW |
| Basic door prop alarms (Detex, Seco-Larm) | ~10% | LOW |

*Source: Expert inference — no published market share exists for this product sub-category. Confidence LOW. Shares are best-estimate placeholders.*

| L4 Subsystem | Description |
|---|---|
| Mechanical Guard Housing | Spring-loaded enclosure mounted over door handle; physically blocks handle depression in locked state |
| Alarm Horn | Piezoelectric 95 dB horn mechanically triggered on guard swing — no electrical power required for activation |
| Wireless Transmitter | 868.3 MHz EasyWave radio module; broadcasts guard-state, alarm, and battery events to receivers up to 30 m (with repeater: 60 m) |
| Battery & Power Management | 9V replaceable block battery; battery status monitoring (optional variant); IoT module draws 9–35 mA during TX |
| Key Override Cylinder | Profile half-cylinder for authorized bypass without alarm trigger |

---

#### L5.h1.3: Video Surveillance & CCTV

IP camera networks providing continuous visual monitoring of campus perimeter, building entries, parking, and interior common areas. Integrated with Video Management Software (VMS) for recording, search, and AI-based analytics.

| Alternative | Market Share | Confidence |
|---|---|---|
| Genetec Security Center VMS | ~28% | MEDIUM |
| Milestone Systems XProtect | ~22% | MEDIUM |
| Axis Communications (cameras + VMS) | ~20% | MEDIUM |
| Avigilon (Motorola Solutions) | ~15% | MEDIUM |
| Hanwha Vision | ~10% | MEDIUM |
| Other | ~5% | LOW |

| L4 Subsystem | Description |
|---|---|
| IP Camera (Fixed / PTZ) | Indoor dome, outdoor varifocal, license plate capture, fisheye panoramic |
| Network Video Recorder (NVR) | On-premise storage; typically 30–90 day retention per campus policy |
| Video Management Software | Live view, motion detection zones, event-triggered playback, VN/access system correlation |
| AI Video Analytics Engine | Person detection, object loitering, crowd density, face recognition (where permissible) |

---

#### L5.h1.4: Fire Detection & Alarm System

Smoke, heat, and CO detection network with voice evacuation capability. Required by NFPA 72 and NFPA 101 for all educational occupancies. Triggers automatic door unlock on activated exits (overrides any access control hold).

| Alternative | Market Share | Confidence |
|---|---|---|
| Notifier / Honeywell Life Safety | ~28% | MEDIUM |
| Siemens (Cerberus / Desigo Fire) | ~22% | MEDIUM |
| Johnson Controls (Simplex) | ~20% | MEDIUM |
| EST / GE / UTC (legacy) | ~15% | MEDIUM |
| Bosch Fire Alarm Systems | ~10% | MEDIUM |
| Other | ~5% | LOW |

| L4 Subsystem | Description |
|---|---|
| Smoke / Heat Detector | Addressable photoelectric, ionization, or heat-of-rate-rise detector per room/zone |
| Pull Station / Manual Call Point | Wall-mounted manual trigger; tested annually per NFPA 72 |
| Fire Alarm Control Panel (FACP) | Central processor; zone mapping, event logging, monitoring center relay |
| Voice Evacuation Amplifier | Intelligible emergency voice instructions per IBC and NFPA 72 |

---

#### L5.h1.5: Mass Notification & Emergency Communication System

Multi-channel emergency alerting — Clery Act mandates timely warning to the campus community. Channels: text/email, outdoor siren, desktop alert, digital signage, PA, social media.

| Alternative | Market Share | Confidence |
|---|---|---|
| Rave Mobile Safety | ~30% | MEDIUM |
| Motorola Solutions (Noggin / Resolver) | ~20% | MEDIUM |
| Omnilert / AlertMedia | ~20% | MEDIUM |
| Federal Signal (outdoor siren) | ~15% | MEDIUM |
| Other | ~15% | LOW |

| L4 Subsystem | Description |
|---|---|
| Outdoor Warning Siren | 360° speaker array; tested monthly; covers outdoor assembly areas |
| Opt-In Alert Database | Student/staff contact records; segmentation by building/zone/cohort |
| Digital Signage Override Interface | Emergency message injection to all campus displays |
| Emergency Operations Center (EOC) Console | Incident command interface; multi-channel activation dashboard |

---

#### L5.h1.6: Physical Security Management Platform (PSIM / GSOC)

Unified software integrating access control, surveillance, fire alarm, and emergency communication event feeds into a single campus security operations view.

| Alternative | Market Share | Confidence |
|---|---|---|
| Lenel OnGuard | ~28% | MEDIUM |
| Genetec Security Center | ~25% | MEDIUM |
| Software House C•CURE 9000 | ~18% | MEDIUM |
| Avigilon Control Center | ~15% | MEDIUM |
| Milestone XProtect (VMS-led PSIM) | ~10% | MEDIUM |
| Other | ~4% | LOW |

| L4 Subsystem | Description |
|---|---|
| Unified Event Feed | Aggregated alarm log from access control, fire, CCTV, and emergency exit monitors |
| GIS / Campus Map Interface | Building floor plans with real-time device status overlay |
| Incident Case Management | Clery Act incident logging, investigation notes, reporting workflow |
| API Integration Layer | REST/SOAP bridges to BMS, emergency notification, IT ticketing |

---

**Read a column:** Community Colleges skip L6c (Research) and have limited L6d activation (fewer residential students). **Read a row:** L6-H1 Campus Safety is universal — every output type is subject to NFPA 101 means of egress requirements and IBC Chapter 10 exit standards, regardless of institution size or type.

---

sections.title: Product Fit: Türwächter in the University VN
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **VN entry level** | L5 |
| **Entry node** | Emergency Exit Alarm Guards (L5.h1.2 under L6-H1) |
| **Architecture distance** | 2 |
| **Already used in market** | true |
| **Türwächter L4 anchor** | Mechanical Guard Housing, Alarm Horn, Wireless Transmitter, Battery & Power Management, Key Override Cylinder |
| **Applicable standards** | NFPA 101 (Life Safety Code) — means of egress; IBC Chapter 10; Clery Act (campus security reporting); EN 179 / EN 1125 (product certifications) |
| **Key constraint fit** | Retrofit-only surface mount (C5) is a STRENGTH in university market: 1/3 of campus buildings are 50+ years old; no-drill installation avoids costly renovation permits. EasyWave protocol lock-in (C6) is the primary friction: campus security platforms (Lenel, Genetec) expect IP-native integration. |
| **Buyer profile** | Director of Public Safety / Campus Security; Facilities Director; Procurement through state or institutional bidding process (public universities: competitive bid required for purchases >$25K threshold) |
| **Sales motion** | Specify-and-install via campus security integrator; or direct to facilities department for pilot (20–50 door retrofit in one building) |
| **Unique advantage** | No-drill surface mount preserves historic building fabric; battery-powered operation avoids conduit runs through 50-year-old walls; 95 dB alarm triggers on guardian swing without power — satisfies NFPA 101 fail-safe requirements |
| **Gap to close** | Campus PSIM integration (Lenel / Genetec gateway required); campus-scale wireless coverage for large lecture/research buildings (repeater chain); US market entry (product currently CE-certified; UL listing needed for US campus procurement) |

---

rows.Value: Deliver accredited higher education programs and credentials to enrolled students within a safe, legally compliant, and research-capable campus environment

Research University (Carnegie R1/R2 — doctoral, extensive research); Teaching-Focused University (M1/M2 — master's, mixed teaching/research); Liberal Arts College (Bac — undergraduate-only); Community / Junior College (associate degrees, certificates); Professional / Technical School (law, medicine, engineering, vocational)
Process VN only (L7 → L4). No Product BOM — output is an educational outcome and credential.
~5,300 colleges and universities in the US; ~19.7M students enrolled; 1/3 of buildings are 50+ years old; national deferred maintenance backlog >$100B (growing at 6–8% annually)
Public research universities (Big Ten, UC System), private liberal arts colleges (NESCAC, LAC networks), community college districts, for-profit professional schools (Strayer, DeVry)

Global school/campus security: USD 4.2B (2025). Higher education segment ~35% of total. Access control global market: USD 10.62B (2025), CAGR 8.3%. Panic & Emergency Exit Devices globally: USD 3.9B (2025). University building age (avg. 50+ years) is the primary retrofit demand driver.
2 (Rank 8 of 15 markets) — product fits standard campus doors without hardware modification; adaptations limited to campus security platform integration and range for large buildings

rows.Name: Admissions & Enrollment
rows.Job Family: Recruit and matriculate qualified applicants into accredited degree programs
rows.Description: Prospecting, application review, financial aid packaging, enrollment confirmation, student record creation, cohort planning

rows.Name: Academic Instruction & Curriculum Delivery
rows.Job Family: Deliver accredited coursework and degree-program learning to enrolled students
rows.Description: Classroom instruction, seminars, online/hybrid delivery, laboratory sessions, studio and clinical instruction, academic advising

rows.Name: Research & Scholarship
rows.Job Family: Generate, validate, and disseminate new knowledge funded by grants and institutional resources
rows.Description: Grant-funded lab research, publication, IP development, graduate training, research compliance (IRB, IACUC)

rows.Name: Student Support & Campus Life
rows.Job Family: Sustain student retention, wellbeing, and social development throughout enrollment
rows.Description: Residence life, counseling, health services, career advising, disability services, dining, recreational facilities

rows.Name: Assessment, Credentialing & Graduation
rows.Job Family: Evaluate academic performance and confer accredited degrees, certificates, and credentials
rows.Description: Grading, degree audit, registrar services, commencement, transcript issuance, alumni records

rows.Name: Campus Safety, Security & Emergency Management (H)
rows.Job Family: Protect all occupants and property across all campus operations while maintaining regulatory-compliant evacuation readiness at all times
rows.Description: Access control, emergency exit monitoring, surveillance, fire detection, mass notification, lockdown systems, Clery Act compliance documentation

rows.Name: Facilities & Physical Plant Management (H)
rows.Job Family: Maintain all campus buildings and infrastructure to code and operational readiness
rows.Description: HVAC, electrical, plumbing, custodial, grounds, deferred maintenance programs, ADA compliance, energy management

rows.Name: IT & Digital Infrastructure (H)
rows.Job Family: Provide reliable network, device, software, and data infrastructure for all core steps
rows.Description: Campus LAN/WiFi, data centers, identity management, ERP, cybersecurity, LMS and SIS platforms

rows.Name: Administrative, Finance & Compliance (H)
rows.Job Family: Ensure legal, accreditation, financial, and HR compliance across all university operations
rows.Description: HR/payroll, budget management, accreditation reporting, legal affairs, board governance, insurance, Title IX compliance
Holistic review workflow, rubric scoring, committee routing
Lead capture, communication cadences, yield prediction
Institutional aid calculation, federal aid integration (FAFSA/CPS)
Deposit collection, housing intent, orientation sign-up
Course registration, grade posting, transcript generation
Aid disbursement, billing, payment plan management
Annual federal reporting, enrollment census, completion stats
Cumulative credit audit, graduation eligibility
Syllabi, readings, video lectures, module sequencing
Quizzes, assignments, rubric grading, grade sync to SIS
Forums, group workspaces, peer review, live virtual classrooms
Early alert flags, login frequency, assignment completion rates
75–110" interactive flat panel or ceiling projector
Touch panel for room switching, volume, source selection
Camera + mic array for automatic recording and streaming
Ceiling speakers, hearing loop (ADA compliance)
Spectrometers, centrifuges, PCR machines, microscopes
Chemical containment, biological safety cabinets (BSL-II)
Sensors, DAQ boards, oscilloscopes, LabVIEW systems
Eyewash stations, emergency shower, fire extinguisher (lab-specific)
MRI, electron microscope, mass spectrometer, wind tunnel (domain-specific)
Freezers (-80°C), liquid nitrogen dewars, clean room equipment
Temperature, humidity, vibration isolation for instrument integrity
Fume extraction, gas detection, emergency shutoff
Budget templates, sponsor portal submission, routing/approval
Protocol submission, review board routing, compliance logging
Grant accounting, effort reporting, indirect cost recovery
OFAC/EAR/ITAR screening for foreign national access to research areas
Preference matching, lottery, contract workflow
Work order submission, tracking, resolution logging
Door access credentials linked to room assignment
Conduct case management, RA reporting
SOAP notes, immunization records, prescription management
Provider calendars, self-service booking portal
Session notes, treatment plans, crisis flags, referral tracking
Student health insurance verification, claims management
Requirement blocks, exception workflow, transfer credit mapping
Student-facing scenario planning for major changes
Intent to graduate form, diploma name verification, commencement registration
Live or AI-based video monitoring, browser lockdown
Turnitin, iThenticate — submission comparison against source corpus
Exam terminal lockdown for in-person computer-based testing
Electric strikes, magnetic locks, electromechanical mortise locks; door hardware for each access point
Proximity card (125 kHz / 13.56 MHz), mobile BLE credential, biometric reader
Embedded controller managing request-to-exit, lock/unlock scheduling, audit log
Credential database, zone provisioning, real-time event log, badge issuance
Spring-loaded enclosure mounted over door handle; physically blocks handle depression in locked state
Piezoelectric 95 dB horn mechanically triggered on guard swing — no electrical power required for activation
868.3 MHz EasyWave radio module; broadcasts guard-state, alarm, and battery events to receivers up to 30 m (with repeater: 60 m)
9V replaceable block battery; battery status monitoring (optional variant); IoT module draws 9–35 mA during TX
Profile half-cylinder for authorized bypass without alarm trigger
Indoor dome, outdoor varifocal, license plate capture, fisheye panoramic
On-premise storage; typically 30–90 day retention per campus policy
Live view, motion detection zones, event-triggered playback, VN/access system correlation
Person detection, object loitering, crowd density, face recognition (where permissible)
Addressable photoelectric, ionization, or heat-of-rate-rise detector per room/zone
Wall-mounted manual trigger; tested annually per NFPA 72
Central processor; zone mapping, event logging, monitoring center relay
Intelligible emergency voice instructions per IBC and NFPA 72
360° speaker array; tested monthly; covers outdoor assembly areas
Student/staff contact records; segmentation by building/zone/cohort
Emergency message injection to all campus displays
Incident command interface; multi-channel activation dashboard
Aggregated alarm log from access control, fire, CCTV, and emergency exit monitors
Building floor plans with real-time device status overlay
Clery Act incident logging, investigation notes, reporting workflow
REST/SOAP bridges to BMS, emergency notification, IT ticketing
VAV, AHU, chiller/boiler sequencing; zone temperature setpoints
Daylight harvesting, occupancy-triggered lighting, energy scheduling
Submetering for electricity, steam, chilled water, gas per building
Door unlock relay from BMS on fire alarm; occupancy data from access control
Reactive and preventive work orders; technician dispatch; labor tracking
Equipment nameplate data, warranty, inspection history, depreciation
Time-based and meter-based PM triggers; NFPA 101 inspection reminders
Room inventory, occupancy tracking, lease management
Layer 3 routing; redundant uplinks; VLAN segmentation for security OT
802.11ax (Wi-Fi 6/6E); building coverage for mobile credentials and IoT devices
Device registration, 802.1X, guest network isolation
Inter-campus connectivity; cloud breakout for SaaS platforms
Centralized user provisioning; LDAP / SCIM sync to building access control
SAML/OIDC federation across LMS, SIS, ERP, and campus portals
Required for staff and faculty; opt-in for students; FIDO2 passkey adoption growing
Automated deprovisioning on graduation/departure; access revocation

rows.Value: Emergency Exit Alarm Guards (L5.h1.2 under L6-H1)
Mechanical Guard Housing, Alarm Horn, Wireless Transmitter, Battery & Power Management, Key Override Cylinder
NFPA 101 (Life Safety Code) — means of egress; IBC Chapter 10; Clery Act (campus security reporting); EN 179 / EN 1125 (product certifications)

Retrofit-only surface mount (C5) is a STRENGTH in university market: 1/3 of campus buildings are 50+ years old; no-drill installation avoids costly renovation permits. EasyWave protocol lock-in (C6) is the primary friction: campus security platforms (Lenel, Genetec) expect IP-native integration.

Director of Public Safety / Campus Security; Facilities Director; Procurement through state or institutional bidding process (public universities: competitive bid required for purchases >$25K threshold)
Specify-and-install via campus security integrator; or direct to facilities department for pilot (20–50 door retrofit in one building)

No-drill surface mount preserves historic building fabric; battery-powered operation avoids conduit runs through 50-year-old walls; 95 dB alarm triggers on guardian swing without power — satisfies NFPA 101 fail-safe requirements

Campus PSIM integration (Lenel / Genetec gateway required); campus-scale wireless coverage for large lecture/research buildings (repeater chain); US market entry (product currently CE-certified; UL listing needed for US campus procurement)
